title: referrals — TenancyHook + lifecycle hooks
description: Cross-module hook implementations. TenancyHooks are the tenancy module's contract for reacting to tenant-lifecycle events (provision / suspend / erase). Referrals does not register a bootstrapper (nothing to hydrate at tenant-boot), but registers nine cascade hooks: user-activation code auto-issue, attribution snapshotter, atomic Finance-materialization, refund + chargeback clawback cascades, user-erasure redaction, tenant-erasure purge, and program-archived cascade.

hooks.name: IssueUserReferralCodeOnAccountActivated
hooks.purpose: Auto-issue a user_scoped referral code for the newly-activated user, for each active referral program on the tenant. Dispatches IssueReferralCodeJob. Idempotent: skips programs the user already has a code for. Runs at priority 60 (after user profile is fully populated, before onboarding-email fanout so the welcome mail can include the code).

hooks.name: SnapshotAttributionOnReferralClaimed
hooks.purpose: Reads Request::attributionSnapshot() (from growth::attribution's AttributionSnapshotBuilder) and freezes it into referral.attribution_snapshot JSONB. Runs synchronously inside the create transaction — MUST commit atomically with the referral row.

hooks.name: MaterializeVestedRewardsIntoFinance
hooks.purpose: Dispatches MaterializeVestedRewardsJob for each reward created by the vested transition. The job calls the Finance module (Wave 4) to create a pending Credit. Runs at priority 30 (before notification listeners so notifications reflect materialized state).

hooks.name: ClawbackRewardsOnFinanceRefund
hooks.purpose: Cascades: dispatches ClawbackRewardsOnRefundJob for the refunded user + payment. Reverses any ReferralReward tied to the refund via the trigger_event chain (typically first_payment).
hooks.note: Wave 4 bridge — event ships in Finance module.

hooks.name: ClawbackRewardsOnFinanceChargeback
hooks.purpose: Higher-severity clawback path. Same shape as refund, but marks the referrer's fraud-flag counters (recurring chargeback abuse = fraud signal on the referrer).

hooks.name: CancelReferralsOnReferrerErased
hooks.purpose: When a referrer user is erased: cancel their pending referrals (transition status -> cancelled with cancellation_reason='manual_admin'). Retains paid rewards (financial obligation) with recipient_id nulled.

hooks.name: RedactReferralDataOnUserErasure
hooks.purpose: GDPR minimisation. If erased user was a referred party: 90 days post-erasure, redact referral.referred_email + attribution_snapshot's user-identifying fields to [REDACTED]. Retention window unchanged; only PII fields redact.
hooks.note: Complements CancelReferralsOnReferrerErased — the two hooks handle different sides of the referrer/referred axis.

hooks.name: PurgeReferralDataForErasedTenant
hooks.purpose: Cascade delete via FK; writes compliance audit trail entry + emits count metrics per entity family (programs / codes / referrals / rewards / fraud_flags purged). Paid rewards over $600 (tax threshold) migrate to compliance_archive.referral_rewards prior to cascade. Audit rows outlive the source rows per financial retention.

hooks.name: ArchiveReferralsOnProgramArchived
hooks.purpose: Cascades: cancels every non-terminal referral referencing the archived program (transition status -> cancelled with cancellation_reason='program_archived'). Materialized-not-yet-paid rewards clawback. Paid rewards retain — the archive doesn't undo already-fulfilled obligations. Runs at priority 50 (before notification-tier listeners).

no_bootstrapper.reason: Referrals does not need a tenant-boot hydration path — nothing per-tenant is preloaded into memory at boot. The attribution middleware handles per-request context; the program config is fetched fresh per referral write.
